language.title: Choose Your Language
language.description: Select the language for the setup wizard and default panel language.

database.title: Database Configuration
database.description: Configure the connection to your MySQL database.
database.name: Database Name
database.test_failed: Could not connect to the database: {{error}}
database.docker_detected: Docker environment detected. Database fields have been pre-filled.
database.docker_db_ready: Docker database is already configured and ready. You can proceed to the next step.
database.fresh: Fresh install — drop every existing table in this database before migrating
database.fresh_hint: Tick this if you hit "Table already exists" — the selected database will be wiped. All data in it is lost.

pelican.title: Pelican Panel
pelican.description: Connect Peregrine to your Pelican panel.
pelican.api_key_help: Generate an Application API key (papp_...) in your Pelican admin panel under API Keys.
pelican.client_api_key_help: Generate a Client API key (pacc_...) from your admin account in Pelican. This key is used to proxy server actions (console, files, power) for all users.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: Pick the default sign-in behaviour. Everything else is configurable post-install.

allow_local_registration.label: Allow local registration
allow_local_registration.help: When enabled, new users can create an account with email + password at /register. Turn off if users must come through an OAuth provider only.

auth.post_install_note: OAuth providers (Shop, Paymenter, WHMCS, Google, Discord, LinkedIn) and two-factor authentication are configured after installation in Settings → Auth & Security.

bridge.title: Bridge Module
bridge.description: Automatically provision servers when customers purchase through Stripe.
bridge.disabled_note: You can enable this later in the admin settings.
bridge.stripe_webhook_secret_help: Create a webhook endpoint in your Stripe dashboard pointing to {{url}} and paste the signing secret here.

summary.title: Review Configuration
summary.description: Review your settings before installing Peregrine.

backfill.title: Initial synchronization
backfill.subtitle: Peregrine is importing existing servers, users, eggs and nodes from Pelican. You can keep going through the wizard while it runs.

webhook.verify_failed: The receiver isn't responding correctly. Retry or continue.
webhook.title: Enable real-time sync
webhook.subtitle: Configure Pelican to notify Peregrine live (recommended). You can skip this step — Peregrine will still sync once a day.
webhook.howto_intro: In Pelican, go to /admin/webhooks → Create Webhook and fill in exactly as below.
webhook.headers_label: Headers (Pelican section — click "Add row" to add Authorization)
webhook.headers_hint: The 1st row (X-Webhook-Event = {{event}}) is the Pelican default — leave it. The 2nd row (Authorization) must be ADDED manually via "Add row".
webhook.copy_warning: Copy the token now — it won't be shown in plain text after this step.
webhook.events_to_tick: Events to tick (search for them in the Pelican list)
webhook.do_not_tick: DO NOT tick (flood / loops): event: ActivityLogged, Schedule, Task, ApiKey, Webhook, WebhookConfiguration.
